intro: Adenorandia kalbreyeri, a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, is a type of medicinal plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Adenorandia kalbreyeri include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
